title: Pesmenben IV

'''Pesmenben IV''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Teraab sector/Legends|Teraab sector]] that had [[Ore/Legends|ore]] deposits and dunes. At one point by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the businessman [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] duped the planet's [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Pesmenben IV)|Imperial governor]] into leasing it by pulling a scam involving [[lithium]].

==Description==
Pesmenben IV was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with dunes,<ref name="ROTJ" /> occupying the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in the [[Pesmenben system/Legends|Pesmenben system]].<ref name="CSWE" /> It was located in the [[Teraab sector/Legends|Teraab sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Its [[Ore/Legends|ore]] deposits were superior to those of the nearby planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]].<ref name="Wiz">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:Young_Lando-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Lando Calrissian once operated a lithium scam on Pesmenben IV.]]
At some point by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' establishes that [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] scheme on Pesmenben IV occurred prior to the [[Battle of Ylesia]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] businessman [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] ran a [[lithium]] scam on Pesmenben IV: he and a group of associates salted its dunes with [[lithium carbonate]]<ref name="ROTJ" />—in a similar fashion to how he himself had been duped by a [[berubian]] [[Mining/Legends|mine]] scheme in the [[Borgo asteroid belt]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>—in order to fool its [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Pesmenben IV)|Imperial governor]] into leasing the planet. Calrissian posed as a non-union mine [[Security guard/Legends|guard]] and made the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] lie face down in the bottom of a [[Boat/Legends|boat]], forcing the latter to throw his bribe overboard when the former's associates, the "union officials," raided them. Calrissian and company got away with the Pesmenben job, and he was later reminded of it when posing as a guard in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], hoping that that job would be similarly successful but expecting that the opposing guards there would possibly need to be thrown overboard as well.<ref name="ROTJ" />

Calrissian's scam on Pesmenben IV was mentioned in the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history chronicle ''[[The Essential Chronology]]'' in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> and its later update in [[36 ABY]], [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|''The New Essential Chronology'']].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:James_Kahn.jpg|thumb|right|200px|James Kahn, the creator of Pesmenben IV]]
Pesmenben IV was was created by [[James Kahn]]<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|143|Authors of the Expanded Universe|Authors of the Expanded Universe: Chronicler: James Kahn}}</ref> and introduced via mention in his<ref name="ROTJ" /> [[May 12]], [[1983]]<ref name="YBY">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']],<ref name="ROTJ" /> which was released on [[May 25]] of the same year.<ref name="YBY" /> [[Michael Kogge]]'s article "[[Authors of the Expanded Universe]]: Chronicler: James Kahn,"<ref name="Insider" /> published on [[July 23]], [[2013]]<ref name="InsiderDate">{{TitanMagazines|url=t/star-wars-insider/us/143|text=''Star Wars Insider'' 143}}</ref> in the [[Star Wars Insider 143|143rd issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, revealed that Kahn had named Pesmenben IV after his grandfather—Ben Pesmen—who first inspired him to pursue the arts.<ref name="Insider" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] roleplaying game source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|issue thirteen]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Pesmenben [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Pesmenben IV, in the [[Trebela sector]]; however, as the magazine was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it is considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Pesmenben system in grid square Q-11,<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' placement by establishing the Pesmenben system to have been a part of the Teraab [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />
